events.title: CrossFit Games
events.address: 525 W Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: Parking for the 2026 CrossFit Games Parking Attending Multiple Days? The 3-Day Parking Pass is the recommended option for guests attending more than one day of the CrossFit Games. Enjoy the convenience of a single parking purchase for the entire weekend, guaranteed access to the ABC Lot, and the flexibility to come and go as needed throughout the event. 3-Day Parking Pass (ABC Lot) Guaranteed parking access in the ABC Lot all weekend Unlimited in-and-out privileges throughout the event One parking credential valid for all three event days No need to purchase parking separately each day Exclusive access to the ABC Lot Pricing Advance Purchase: $150 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate (if available): $180 Please note: ABC Lot inventory is limited and may sell out before the event. Advance purchase is strongly recommended. Purchase All 3 Days Vip Parking Package (includes In and Out Privileges in the A-B-C Main Lot) Attending a Single Day? Single-day parking is available in the Delmas Lots. Single-Day Parking Pass (Delmas Lots) Valid only on the selected event day Parking access from 8 AM to 7 PM No in-and-out privileges Re-entry requires a new parking purchase Pricing Advance Purchase: $45 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate: $50 Purchase General Event Parking for Friday, 7/24 Purchase General Event Parking for Saturday, 7/25 Purchase General Event Parking for Sunday 7/26 Why Purchase Parking in Advance? Parking inventory for the CrossFit Games weekend is limited. Purchasing parking before arriving is the easiest way to ensure a smooth and stress-free event experience. Benefits of purchasing in advance: Secure your parking before lots reach capacity Avoid searching for available parking on event day Speed up your arrival experience Guarantee access to your selected parking option Guests attending multiple days are encouraged to purchase the 3-Day Parking Pass early, as ABC Lot inventory is limited.
events.blurb: Watch elite athletes compete in the CrossFit Games.

events.title: This Is Now with Angie Coiro: Casey Gerald
events.address: 1010 El Camino Real, Menlo Park, CA, 94025

events.title: Middle School Bike Skills
events.address: Cupertino Senior Center, 21251 Stevens Creek Blvd, 95014
events.description: Middle School Bike Skills workshops prepare rising 6th, 7th, and 8th graders to bike to middle school and beyond. The original course teaches students and their parent or guardian safe biking fundamentals. New this year is a sequel which teaches students how to adapt those skills to stay safe while riding an e-bike and a third course teaching students the basics of maintaining and doing simple repairs on their own bikes. Msbs 101 - Middle School Bike Workshop Msbs 102 - e-Bike Workshop Msbs 103 - Bike Maintenance and Repair Workshop This Middle School Bike Skills program is brought to you by Cupertino Safe Routes to School in partnership with Wheel Kids. Registration is required. Links and more information can be found at www.cupertino.gov/saferoutes/msbs
events.blurb: You and your middle schooler learn safe biking fundamentals to ride to school confidently.

events.title: Free Home Composting Education Workshop
events.description: The Uc Cooperative Extension Composting Education Program, sponsored by the Recycling and Waste Reduction Commission of Santa Clara County, offers community classes throughout Santa Clara County that teach residents how to build compost piles and worm bins and apply the compost in their landscape. View additional details about these classes on the Home Compost page. Please note: Composting Basics Workshop scheduled for April 11 is canceled due to rain. Time and location change for the September 26 date.
events.blurb: Learn to build compost piles and worm bins, then use finished compost in your garden.

events.title: Monthly Chat with Mayor Moore

events.title: The Employee Dharma Handbook
events.description: As an important rocket launch approaches, Val, an HR Executive at an aerospace company in Silicon Valley, suspects that a young engineer was overlooked for a promotion. Leela, a first generation Indian-American, doesn't really want to talk about it, but Val suspects sexism is the cause. What they don't expect to uncover are simmering tensions of ancient origins among the company's Indian immigrant employees…sorry, Valued Team Members. Soon Val is navigating a funhouse of identity, nepotism, and desire as she tries to understand the culture and biases of Leela's managers. When push comes to shove, Val will need to decide what her duty is to Leela, to the company, and to herself.
events.blurb: Watch a play about an HR executive navigating workplace bias at an aerospace company during a pivotal rocket launch.

events.description: As an important rocket launch approaches, Val, an HR Executive at an aerospace company in Silicon Valley, suspects that a young engineer was overlooked for a promotion. Leela, a first generation Indian-American, doesn't really want to talk about it, but Val suspects sexism is the cause. What they don't expect to uncover are simmering tensions of ancient origins among the company's Indian immigrant employees…sorry, Valued Team Members. Soon Val is navigating a funhouse of identity, nepotism, and desire as she tries to understand the culture and biases of Leela's managers. When push comes to shove, Val will need to decide what her duty is to Leela, to the company, and to herself.
events.blurb: Watch a play about an HR executive navigating workplace bias at an aerospace company during a pivotal rocket launch.

events.title: Pooch Plunge
events.address: Blackberry Farm Park, 21979 San Fernando Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Let your pup take the plunge during a dog-only swim in the Blackberry Farm pools. It's a paws-itively fun way for your pooch to enjoy the pools before they open for summer swimming—tail wags are highly encouraged. Swim Sessions (45 minutes of tail-wagging fun) Block 1 - 9 to 9:45 a.m. Block 2 - 10 to 10:45 a.m. Block 3 - 11 to 11:45 a.m. Block 4 - Noon to 12:45 p.m. Register Registration opens in February for the spring event and in June for the fall event. For additional information, visit the Pooch Plunge webpage.
events.blurb: Your dog swims solo in the pool during a dog-only splash session before summer opens.

events.title: Dracula
events.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Sink your teeth into the ultimate Halloween thrill. Steven Dietz's adaptation of the timeless classic seduces with elegance and suspense, turning the legendary vampire tale into a high-octane theatrical ride. Bold, chilling, and wickedly smart, the story draws you into a world where reason clashes with superstition and love becomes dangerously intoxicating. This Dracula keeps you leaning forward—never quite sure who holds the power, or what darkness waits just out of sight.
events.blurb: Witness the gothic horror classic rendered as a compelling stage production with talented actors.

events.title: Monster Mash
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Get ready for a fang-tastic good time at Cupertino Parks and Recreation's annual Monster Mash Halloween event at the Quinlan Community Center. Put on your favorite costume and enjoy an evening of games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating. It's a fun-filled night of Halloween activities for the whole family. Register Registration for Monster Mash begins in late July.
events.blurb: Wear your costume and enjoy games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wildlife Tracking
events.description: Join a Park Ranger for a fun-filled wildlife tracking class. You'll learn about species identification through their tracks and sign-tracking. Become a nature detective and learn to read the ground like a storybook through the art of tracking. Please wear sturdy walking shoes and long pants that can get dirty. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult.
events.blurb: Learn to identify animal tracks and read the ground like a nature detective with a park ranger.

events.title: Don Giovanni
events.description: DON Giovanni Back after 12 years, Opera San Jose builds a new production of Mozart's dark psychological thriller. Seduction, rebellion, and supernatural justice collide in a thrilling look at a notorious libertine's downfall. The production follows a charismatic anti-hero who pursues pleasure without remorse, leaving a trail of broken hearts and vengeful spirits in a […]
events.blurb: Watch Mozart's dark psychological thriller about a libertine's seduction, rebellion, and supernatural downfall.

events.title: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino
events.description: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino – meaning island sounds in Cupertino Experience the rhythms, songs, and dances of the islands! Na Leo Mokupuni brings together performers to celebrate the vibrant traditions of island music and dance—right here in Cupertino. Preregistration is recommended. Limited tickets available at the door, while supplies last.
events.blurb: Experience island rhythms, songs, and dances rooted in Hawaiian and Pacific island traditions.

events.title: Cupertino Campout
events.address: Creekside Park, 10455 Miller Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: The Cupertino Campout is a weekend to remember! Families are invited to pitch a tent at Creekside Park and join us for a night under the stars, filled with recreational activities. This campout is a fantastic way to gather with family, neighbors, and friends or make new ones, all while enjoying all kinds of fun. Pre-registration is required.
events.blurb: Pitch a tent and spend the night under the stars with recreational activities and neighbors.

events.title: Breakfast with Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: You and your family are invited to a festive breakfast celebration with Santa! Enjoy delicious North Pole pancakes prepared by Santa's elves, take photos with Santa, and explore a holiday workshop filled with crafts and activities for children. Three seatings: 8:30 a.m., 9:30 a.m., 10:30 a.m. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Enjoy pancakes with Santa, take photos, and do holiday crafts with your family.

events.title: 2026 Cupertino Community Service Awards
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 N. Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: The third annual Cupertino Community Service Awards will take place on Wednesday, October 28. This ceremony will recognize the 2026 Crest Awards (Cupertino Recognizes Extra Steps Taken), and Commissioners who served on a City Commission. More details and registration information for the October 28 event will be available soon.
events.blurb: Watch the Cupertino Community Service Awards ceremony honoring local volunteers and commissioners.

events.title: Environmental Education Center Open House
events.description: Drop by the Environmental Education Center (EEC) at Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve every Saturday from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. for family fun and discovery. Meet the City's animal ambassadors, explore local wildlife, engage with preserve staff and park rangers, and enjoy hands-on activities that bring nature to life.
events.blurb: Meet animal ambassadors, learn about local wildlife, and do hands-on activities with park rangers.

events.title: Signing Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: Deaf and hard of hearing children, along with their families, are invited to spend time with a Santa who understands sign language! Santa only communicates using sign language but an interpreter will be on-site to help translate for friends and family. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Meet a signing Santa who communicates entirely in ASL with your deaf or hard-of-hearing children.

events.title: Free Shakespeare in the Park Event
events.description: Free Shakespeare in the Park brings you Anthony and Cleopatra directed by Katja Rivera. Set against the backdrop of the Roman Empire and Egypt, For show details, Anthony and Cleopatra examines the conflict between duty and desire, and the impact of personal relationships. Visit sfshakes.org.
events.blurb: Watch Shakespeare's Anthony and Cleopatra performed outdoors in the amphitheater.

events.title: Star-Spangled Day of Play
events.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
events.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
events.description: A festive patriotic celebration featuring sparkler-inspired art creation, patriotic decorations throughout the museum, and Bubble Wrap Firecracker Stomp sessions at 12:00 pm and 1:00 pm in Central Park with projected fireworks on the indoor theatre screen. Included with museum admission.
events.blurb: Make sparkler-inspired art, stomp on bubble wrap firecrackers, and watch projected fireworks.

events.title: Guided Meditation & Breath Awareness
events.address: Central Park Library - Redwood Room, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: A beginner-friendly guided meditation and breath awareness session designed to help participants relax and reconnect with the present moment. The program includes guided meditation, gentle joint rotations, and light movements to support relaxation. No registration required.
events.blurb: Join a beginner-friendly guided meditation and breath awareness session.

events.title: Tree Lighting
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Kick off the holiday season at the City's annual Community Tree Lighting! Enjoy the moment the tree is illuminated with hundreds of twinkling lights, live music from a local choir, and festive holiday cheer. After the tree lighting, families can visit Santa and take photos.
events.blurb: Watch the holiday tree light up, hear a local choir sing, and visit Santa for photos.

events.title: Summer BBQ Social
events.address: Rengstorff Park BBQ Area, Mountain View, CA
events.description: Mid-summer celebration featuring hot dogs, fruit, chips, and a sweet treat. Adults 55+ welcome; younger guests must be accompanied by an adult 55+. Pre-registration required; tickets $5 per person (cash only) sold at Mountain View Senior Center Front Desk June 1–26.
events.blurb: Eat hot dogs, fruit, chips, and dessert at a mid-summer gathering for adults 55-plus.

events.title: Knitting & Crochet Club
events.address: Central Park Library, Central Studio, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: A casual, self-directed social group where community members gather to work on their own knitting or crochet projects, share inspiration, and meet others who enjoy yarn crafts. All experience levels welcome; no instruction provided and no registration required.
events.blurb: Knit or crochet alongside other crafters, share projects, and connect over yarn work.

events.title: 61st Annual Campbell Chamber Foundation Golf Tournament
events.address: Cinnabar Hills Golf Club, 23600 McKean Rd, San Jose, CA
events.description: Golf tournament featuring a scramble format open to seasoned golfers and beginners. Includes lunch, putting contest, 18 holes of golf, and an evening buffet dinner with awards ceremony and live auctions. Supports the Campbell Chamber Community Foundation.

events.title: Baer Blacksmith Shop Demonstrations
events.description: Community members are invited to visit the Baer Blacksmith Shop from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. and learn about early Cupertino history, the craft of blacksmithing, and learn from our experienced blacksmith, who will be firing the forge to give demonstrations.
events.blurb: Watch a blacksmith fire the forge and demonstrate the craft while learning early Cupertino history.

events.title: Triton Online: Exploring Acrylic Ocean Painting
events.description: Six-week online acrylic painting class focusing on ocean landscapes with artist Jeff Bramschreiber. Students may use provided references or their own subjects.
events.blurb: Paint ocean landscapes in acrylic online with artist Jeff Bramschreiber over six weeks.

events.title: Ms. Holmes & Ms. Watson – Apt 2B
events.address: Montalvo Arts Center, 15400 Montalvo Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070
events.description: A darkly comic modern take on Sherlock Holmes and Watson as oddball female roommates solving mysteries. Part of the Play on the Grounds outdoor theater series.

events.title: California Clasico with fireworks
events.description: San Jose Earthquakes face LA Galaxy in the 105th California Clasico with fireworks celebrating America's 250th birthday. Four Upper Midfield tickets for $120.

events.title: Bora Lee Reed with Natalie Baszile
events.description: Bora Lee Reed joins us to discuss Song for Another Home, her Pachinko -meets- Homegoing story of a family's separation and reunion amid love and war in Korea.
events.blurb: Hear author Bora Lee Reed discuss her novel about a Korean family's separation and reunion.

events.title: All Ages Storytime: Memoirs of a Goldfish
events.venue: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
events.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, Brandenburg Theatre, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
events.description: A story reading of Memoirs of a Goldfish by Devin Scillian, featuring a goldfish whose solitary life is disrupted when assorted companions invade his bowl.
events.blurb: Hear the story of a goldfish whose quiet life changes when new companions arrive in his bowl.

events.title: New! Ma in Social Impact Leadership Informational Session
events.description: Interested in leading complex organizations to build a more humane, just and sustainable world? Consider the MA in Social Impact Leadership program at SCU!
events.blurb: Hear about SCU's MA in Social Impact Leadership program and how to lead organizations toward justice.

events.title: Michael Moritz with Adam Lashinsky
events.description: Michael Moritz joins us with his deeply moving family story of displacement and otherness in the wake of the Holocaust—a legacy that must be heeded today.
events.blurb: Hear Michael Moritz share a family story of displacement and resilience after the Holocaust.

events.title: Sacramento Fireside Chat with Former State Senator Bob Wieckowski J.D. '85
events.description: Join State Senator Bob Wieckowski, J. D. '85 for a fireside chat. Enjoy a conversation on the importance of public service and being a leader for others.
events.blurb: Hear State Senator Bob Wieckowski discuss public service and leadership.

events.title: Night Market #3
events.description: Community Night Market featuring food vendors, live music, dancing, and cultural performances celebrating Santa Clara's culinary and cultural diversity.
events.blurb: Eat from food vendors, dance to live music, and enjoy cultural performances celebrating Santa Clara.

events.title: Fourth of July Fireworks Spectacular
events.address: Shoreline Amphitheatre, 1 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA
events.description: Fireworks celebration featuring the San Francisco Symphony performing 'Summer of 1969: The Soundtrack of a Generation' before the fireworks display.
events.blurb: Watch the San Francisco Symphony perform Summer of 1969 before the fireworks display begins.

events.title: Marketing Mondays: Google Business Profile
events.description: Workshop teaching local SEO optimization, Google Business Profile management, review strategies, and use of Posts to increase community visibility.
events.blurb: Learn to optimize your Google Business Profile, manage reviews, and boost local search visibility.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Match 81: Round of 32
events.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 match featuring the winner of Group D versus a wildcard team, one of six World Cup matches hosted in Santa Clara.
events.blurb: Watch a FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 match between Group D winners and a wildcard team.

events.title: Building brain-healthy habits
events.description: Learn about brain-healthy habits like exercise and nutrition, the brain-heart connection, and build a personalized action plan for brain health.
events.blurb: Learn about exercise, nutrition, and brain-heart health, then build your own personalized brain health action plan.

events.title: Silverpoint under the trees
events.address: Redwoods, 1505 Warburton Avenue, Santa Clara, CA
events.description: Workshop learning the ancient art of silverpoint drawing in the museum's redwood grove with fine artist Jeff Bramschreiber. All levels welcome.
events.blurb: Learn the ancient technique of silverpoint drawing in Redwoods' grove with fine artist Jeff Bramschreiber.

events.title: Buck Full Moon Group Hike
events.address: Alum Rock Park, San Jose, CA
events.description: Full moon group hike featuring stretching, connection circle, snacks, and a 7-mile moderate loop. Experience golden hour into moonlight hiking.

events.title: Spotlight Tours Thursdays
events.description: Join a 15-minute spotlight exploring one object in the Anderson Collection. New works are featured each week! Meet at the top of the stairs.
events.blurb: Join a guided 15-minute tour of a featured artwork in the Anderson Collection.

events.title: Encanto en Familia: Santa Clara Legacy Latinx Picnic
events.description: Join your SCU familia for an afternoon of raffles, community, games, and celebration at the fourth annual Encanto en Familia Latinx Picnic!
events.blurb: Play games, win raffle prizes, and celebrate Latinx community with Santa Clara students.

events.title: Star-Spangled Day of Play
events.address: Children's Discovery Museum, 180 Woz Way, San Jose, CA 95110
events.description: Festive day of red, white, and blue activities including sparkler-inspired art and Bubble Wrap Firecracker Stomp with projected fireworks.
events.blurb: Make patriotic crafts and stomp on Bubble Wrap to fireworks projections at the museum.

events.title: Watercolor Painting 4-Week Class for Beginners
events.description: Professional artist-led class teaching watercolor fundamentals including brush handling, washes, wet-on-wet, and dry brushing techniques.
events.blurb: Learn watercolor brush handling, washes, and wet techniques in a four-week beginner class.

events.title: Triton Book Club: The Illustrated Man by Ray Bradbury
events.address: Triton Museum of Art, 1505 Warburton Avenue, Santa Clara, CA
events.description: Triton Curator Vanessa Callanta tours the history of tattoo art and body art across cultures through Bradbury's science fiction classic.
events.blurb: Hear curator Vanessa Callanta explore tattoo and body art history across cultures through Ray Bradbury's sci-fi classic.

events.title: Reading with Pets
events.address: Central Park Library - Redwood Room, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: Kids in grades K-5 practice reading aloud to quiet therapy pets at the library to improve reading skills, pronunciation, and confidence.
events.blurb: Read aloud to therapy pets at the library to build reading skills, pronunciation, and confidence.

events.title: Triton Online: Explorations in Drawing
events.description: Six-week online drawing class with artist Jeff Bramschreiber featuring weekly references and exploration of various drawing techniques.
events.blurb: Complete a six-week online drawing class exploring various techniques with artist Jeff Bramschreiber.

events.title: Monthly Craft: DIY Folding Fan
events.address: New Museum Los Gatos, 106 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA
events.description: Drop-in, self-led craft activity free with museum admission. Visitors of all ages create a DIY folding fan during regular museum hours.

events.title: Monthly Luncheon with Suzanne Saint-John Crane
events.description: Director of Strategy for Friends of Levitt Pavilion San Jose discusses the vision and journey behind the 2.6-acre live music campus.
events.blurb: Hear how the Levitt Pavilion's director shaped a 2.6-acre live music campus from vision to reality.

events.title: Mayor's Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market
events.description: The Mayor invites you to join Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market the first Saturday of every month, from 10:00 AM to Noon.
events.blurb: Meet the mayor at the farmers' market to discuss community concerns and ideas.

events.title: Monthly Craft: Paper Tents
events.address: New Museum Los Gatos, 106 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA
events.description: Drop-in, self-led craft activity free with museum admission. Visitors of all ages create paper tents during regular museum hours.
events.blurb: Fold and decorate your own paper tent during museum hours.

events.title: Free Museum Day
events.address: Campbell Historical Museum & Ainsley House, Campbell
events.description: Complimentary admission to both museums to explore hands-on exhibits, local stories, and a beautifully preserved historic home.
events.blurb: Tour two museums free: see hands-on exhibits, local stories, and a preserved historic home.

events.title: Joss Richard and Jessica Joyce: Let's Kiss and Tell
events.description: Author event with Joss Richard and Jessica Joyce at Books Inc. Mountain View. Tickets include admission and a copy of the book.

events.title: Community & Climate: The Problem with Plastic
events.description: Author talk with Susan Keefe about plastic pollution, part of the Community & Climate lecture series on global climate action.
events.blurb: Hear author Susan Keefe discuss plastic pollution and climate action.

events.title: American Cancer Society Relay for Life
events.address: Campbell Community Center Track, Campbell, CA
events.description: 24-hour community relay event benefiting the American Cancer Society. Join the Chamber team, make a donation, or volunteer.
events.blurb: Participate in the 24-hour relay event, join a team, donate, or volunteer for cancer research.

events.title: Kengele the African spurred tortoise's 60th birthday celebration
events.address: Happy Hollow Park & Zoo, 1300 Senter Road, San Jose, CA 95112
events.description: Kengele roaming in the meadow and keeper chat at African spurred tortoise habitat celebrating the tortoise's 60th birthday.

events.title: Donatello the capybara's 3rd birthday celebration
events.address: Happy Hollow Park & Zoo, 1300 Senter Road, San Jose, CA 95112
events.description: Capybara keeper chat with cake for Donatello and capybara-themed craft activities celebrating the capybara's 3rd birthday.
events.blurb: Chat with capybara keepers, decorate crafts, and celebrate Donatello's birthday with cake at the zoo.

events.title: Well-being Walk
events.description: Join Peer Health Educators for a campus walk using the SJSU interactive Map! All walks begin in the SWC Wellness Lounge.
events.blurb: Walk the SJSU campus with peer educators starting from the Wellness Lounge.

events.title: Rengstorff Pool Trip
events.address: The View Teen Center (departure point), 263 Escuela Ave, Mountain View, CA 94040
events.description: Afternoon outing to Rengstorff Pool with admission and refreshing snacks included, departing from The View Teen Center.
events.blurb: Swim at Rengstorff Pool with admission and snacks included in this afternoon outing.

events.title: Red, White and Blue Fruit Skewers
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day by making festive red, white and blue fruit skewers. Ages 3 and up. Registration required.
events.blurb: Assemble red, white and blue fruit skewers to celebrate Independence Day.

events.title: Tortellini the desert tortoise's 35th birthday celebration
events.address: Happy Hollow Park & Zoo, 1300 Senter Road, San Jose, CA 95112
events.description: Meet and greet with Tortellini and tortoise-themed craft activities celebrating the desert tortoise's 35th birthday.
events.blurb: Meet Tortellini the desert tortoise, make tortoise-themed crafts, and mark her 35th birthday.

events.title: Summer Luncheons
events.address: Montalvo Arts Center, 15400 Montalvo Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070
events.description: Al fresco lunch on the veranda with live piano music during summer afternoons. Held Wednesdays in July and August.
events.blurb: Eat lunch on the veranda with live piano music during summer afternoons on Wednesdays.

events.title: Fireworks display
events.address: Hyde Middle School, 19325 Bollinger Rd., Cupertino, CA
events.description: Professional fireworks display. View from Creekside Park, Sedgwick Elementary, or Miller Avenue near Bollinger Rd.
events.blurb: Watch a professional fireworks display from Creekside Park or nearby viewing spots.

events.title: Sunnyvale's July 4th Celebration
events.description: Celebrate America's 250th anniversary with live music, a Ferris wheel, amusement rides and more on Murphy Avenue.
events.blurb: Hear live music, ride the Ferris wheel and amusement rides, and celebrate America's 250th anniversary.

events.title: Campbell Relay for Life 2026
events.description: Largest and most inspiring cancer fundraising event supporting research, patient services, and support programs.
events.blurb: Walk and fundraise in the community's largest cancer support event benefiting research and care.

events.title: 70th Anniversary Celebration
events.description: Celebrate 70 years of Saratoga at this free community event honoring the City's history, people, and spirit.
events.blurb: Celebrate Saratoga's 70-year history with local food, activities, and community performances.

events.title: 2026 Campbell Summer Concert Series
events.description: Eight-concert season featuring a blend of old favorites and acts new to the Campbell Summer Concert Series.
events.blurb: Catch eight concerts featuring returning favorites and new acts downtown all summer.

events.title: Disney's Newsies
events.description: High-energy musical about scrappy New York newsboys with energetic dancing and powerful vocal performances.
events.blurb: Watch energetic dancing and powerful vocals in this high-energy musical about New York newsboys.

events.title: San Jose Giants vs Fresno Grizzlies
events.address: Excite Ballpark, 588 East Alma Avenue, San José, CA 95112
events.description: Baseball game with post-game fireworks. SJMA receives a portion of ticket proceeds when code ART26 is used.

events.title: Toddler Time with Farmer Arann
events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.
events.blurb: Sing and dance with a special music performer alongside other young kids.

events.title: Toddler Time with Megan Schoenbohm
events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.
events.blurb: Sing and dance with performer Megan Schoenbohm in this drop-in music session.

events.title: Toddler Time with Mariela's Music Time (bilingual Spanish and English)
events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.
events.blurb: Sing and dance with bilingual music performer Mariela in this drop-in session.

events.title: Toddler Time with Malinky Music (bilingual Spanish and English)
events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.
events.blurb: Sing and dance with a bilingual music performer in this drop-in concert for toddlers.

events.title: Toddler Time with JAMaROO (bilingual Mandarin/Cantonese and English)
events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.
events.blurb: Sing and dance to live music in Mandarin, Cantonese, and English with toddlers.

events.title: Power Networking Lunch
events.description: Chamber networking lunch featuring burritos and street tacos from El Guapo's. Bring your business card.
events.blurb: Eat burritos and tacos from El Guapo's while networking with other professionals over lunch.

events.title: Eloise the collared peccary's 21st birthday celebration
events.address: Happy Hollow Park & Zoo, 1300 Senter Road, San Jose, CA 95112
events.description: Keeper chat with birthday piñata enrichment and peccary keeper chat celebrating Eloise's 21st birthday.
events.blurb: Talk with peccary keepers, watch Eloise's birthday piñata enrichment, and celebrate her 21st.

events.title: Bayou the American alligator's 14th gotcha day celebration
events.address: Happy Hollow Park & Zoo, 1300 Senter Road, San Jose, CA 95112
events.description: Melon feeding, keeper chats and alligator-themed craft activities celebrating Bayou's 14th gotcha day.
events.blurb: Watch keepers feed melons to Bayou the alligator, chat with experts, and make alligator crafts.

events.title: ESL Book Club
events.description: English language book club discussing 'Because of Mr. Terupt.' Open to all, no registration required.
events.blurb: Discuss Because of Mr. Terupt with fellow English learners at this open book club.

events.title: Canine Champions for Conservation
events.address: Happy Hollow Park & Zoo, 1300 Senter Road, San Jose, CA 95112
events.description: Stunt dog show showcasing incredible jumps, tricks and performances. Included with park admission.
events.blurb: Watch stunt dogs perform incredible jumps and tricks in this action-packed show included with admission.

events.title: Mayor's Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market
events.description: The Mayor invites you to join Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market from 10:00 AM to Noon.
events.blurb: Meet the mayor at the farmers' market to discuss community concerns and ideas.

events.title: School-Age S.T.E.M. Days: Things that Creep Live Reptile Presentation
events.description: Drop-in science activity: live reptile presentation (bilingual Spanish and English). Grades 1-5.
events.blurb: Watch live reptiles up close and learn how they move in this bilingual hands-on science session.

events.title: The Megatones - Concerts in the Park
events.description: Live music featuring classic rock, pop, and R&B hits with high-energy, danceable performances.
events.blurb: Dance to classic rock, pop, and R&B hits with the Megatones.

events.title: Backstage Pass: A Museum Collection Behind the Scenes
events.description: Step backstage to see museum spaces normally closed to the public! A museum member exclusive!
events.blurb: Tour museum storage spaces and see works normally kept behind the scenes.

events.title: Family Fun Nights: Magic with Professor Pepper
events.address: Washington Park, 840 W. Washington Ave, Sunnyvale
events.description: Evening of family fun with magic performance (bilingual Spanish and English). For all ages.
events.blurb: Watch Professor Pepper perform magic tricks in Spanish and English for the whole family.
